diningOut.note: diverse restaurant scene; pricier than most LATAM cities

transit.note: Metro + Transantiago bus; good public transit coverage

coworking.note: hot-desk monthly; strong professional coworking ecosystem

misc.note: gym, toiletries, entertainment, Andes weekend trips, buffer

accomSeasonality._doc: 12 monthly multipliers for RENT ONLY (Jan..Dec). 1.0 = base. Southern hemisphere; slight summer demand from internal migration.

evidence.quote: For a digital nomad looking for a studio or one-bedroom apartment, average monthly expenses would be $1,350–1,600; budget around $1,300 to $1,700 USD per month
In Providencia, furnished one-bedroom apartments cost $600–950 per month, while in Las Condes they range $800–1,300
Groceries run $250 to $350 per month. Internet is fast and cheap at $20 to $25 per month for 100+ Mbps

evidence.claim: Santiago pricier than Buenos Aires/Lima but offers high quality + stability
evidence.quote: Santiago is not the most affordable destination in the region, and it tends to be more expensive than cities like Buenos Aires or Lima. However, it offers a high quality of life and solid legal stability
